---
title: ""
page-layout: full
toc: false
listing:
  id: biases-listing
  contents: biases
  type: grid
  sort: "title asc"
  grid-columns: 4
  image-height: "180px"
  fields: [image, title, description]
  page-size: 999
  exclude:
    filename: ["availability-heuristic.qmd", "choices.qmd", "frame-dependence.qmd", "heuristics.qmd", "mental-accounting.qmd", "preference-reversals.qmd", "representativeness.qmd", "social.qmd"]
---

```{=html}
<section class="hero-section">
  <h1>The Effect of Behavioural Biases on Financial Decisions</h1>
  <p class="subtitle">A collection of cognitive biases and their influence on financial decision-making</p>

  <!-- Cloned Quarto search widget, centered in the hero -->
  <div class="hero-search">
    <div id="quarto-search" class="type-overlay" title="Search"></div>
  </div>

  <div class="hero-tags">
    <a class="hero-tag" href="biases/heuristics.qmd">Heuristics and Judgemental Biases</a>
    <a class="hero-tag" href="biases/choices.qmd">Choices: Framing and References</a>
    <a class="hero-tag" href="biases/social.qmd">Social Factors</a>
    <a class="hero-tag" href="biases.qmd">All Biases</a>
  </div>
</section>
```

## What are Behavioural Biases?

Behavioural biases try to explain our tendency to reason in certain ways that can lead to systematic errors or deviations from a standard of rationality or good judgment in thinking or reasoning. In the financial setting, they aim to explain why consumers and investors make decisions that oppose the [standard model of rational choice](https://web.stanford.edu/~jdlevin/Econ%20202/Choice%20Theory.pdf), which states that when presented with a set of options, individuals choose the option that maximizes their expected utility.

This site serves as a catalogue of {{< var n_biases >}} biases across three major categories, in line with the taxonomy proposed by [David Peon and Manel Antelo](https://hemeroteca.unad.edu.co/index.php/revista-estrategica-organizacio/article/view/4963/4705), including some examples from daily life for each.

---

## Browse by Category

```{=html}
<div class="bias-grid">
  <a class="bias-card" href="biases/heuristics.qmd">
    <h3>Heuristics and Judgemental Biases</h3>
    <p>Heuristics are mental "rules of thumb" used to simplify complex decision-making, distorting risk assessment and probability judgement.</p>
  </a>
  <a class="bias-card" href="biases/choices.qmd">
    <h3>Choices: Framing and References</h3>
    <p>How options are presented (framed) changes what we choose. Frame dependence, context effects, mental accounting, and preference reversals.</p>
  </a>
  <a class="bias-card" href="biases/social.qmd">
    <h3>Social Factors</h3>
    <p>Cultural and social factors affect our decision-making, leading to conformity, groupthink and herd mentality.</p>
  </a>
</div>
```
---

## All Biases

::: {#examples-listing}
:::
